mnemonics.title: Thread State Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: NEW → RUNNABLE → (BLOCKED/WAITING/TIMED_WAITING) → TERMINATED. Remember: New Runners Block, Wait, Then Terminate!

mnemonics.title: Waiting vs Timed Waiting
mnemonics.content: WAITING = wait(), join(), LockSupport.park() - INDEFINITE. TIMED_WAITING = sleep(), wait(time), join(time), LockSupport.parkNanos() - HAS TIME LIMIT.

mnemonics.title: Blocked vs Waiting
mnemonics.content: BLOCKED = Waiting for MONITOR lock (synchronized). WAITING = Waiting for NOTIFY or condition (Object.wait, Thread.join, Lock).

acronyms.expansion: NEW-RUNNABLE-BLOCKED-WAITING-TERMINATED - The 5 main thread states (TIMED_WAITING is timed version of WAITING)
acronyms.usage: Remember this acronym for the 5-6 states in thread lifecycle

acronyms.expansion: Sleep, Wait, notify - Methods that change thread state
acronyms.usage: SLeep → TIMED_WAITING, Waits → WAITING, notify → back to RUNNABLE

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle with arrows: NEW → start() → RUNNABLE → (sleep→TIMED_WAITING→timeout→RUNNABLE) OR (wait→WAITING→notify→RUNNABLE) OR (blocked→lock→RUNNABLE) → run() ends → TERMINATED
Think of thread like a person: NEW = Born, RUNNABLE = Working/Ready, BLOCKED = Stuck at door, WAITING = Taking a break forever, TIMED_WAITING = Coffee break (timer), TERMINATED = Retired/Done
Three queues at a mall: BLOCKED = People waiting to enter (one door), WAITING = VIP lounge waiting for personal call, TIMED_WAITING = People with appointment timer

analogies.analogy: Think of a thread like a student in school: NEW = Not yet admitted, RUNNABLE = In class and learning, BLOCKED = Stuck in traffic trying to get to school, WAITING = Waiting for parent to pick up (indefinite), TIMED_WAITING = Waiting for bus with schedule, TERMINATED = Graduated/Done
getState() is like a security camera - it shows you the current snapshot of what the thread is doing right now, but can change any moment
BLOCKED is like waiting for a bathroom in a crowded mall - you can only enter when someone comes out and the door is unlocked (monitor lock available)
